Measles, Cholera, and Mpox: BU-Based Outbreak Tracker Monitors World’s Most Dangerous Infectious Diseases
articles.description: Last year alone, more than 500,000 people globally were sickened by cholera—and 6,500 died. Chikungunya, a mosquito-spread disease that can cause disabling joint pain, afflicted a similar number, though more patients survived. And measles, once considered eliminated in many countries, came roaring back, reaching levels in the United States not seen in over three decades....

articles.title: Signs of ‘feeding’ ritual at dingo burial site shed new light on bond between First Nations people and canines
articles.description: Never documented archaeologically before, evidence points to First Nations people caring for and nursing the animal Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The discovery of a millennium-old dingo burial site in western New South Wales, including evidence of a “feeding” ritual never before documented archaeologically, has shed new light on the longstanding relationship between the canines and First Nations people. The dingo was buried along the Baaka, or Darling River, in Kinchega national park near the Menindee Lakes. Continue reading...

articles.title: Occidental Mindoro town forecasts heat index of 45°C
articles.description: CALAPAN CITY, Oriental Mindoro — The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) of San Jose, Occidental Mindoro, warned on Monday that the town’s heat index could reach 45°C, as Calapan City hits an “extreme caution” reading of 40°C. The City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Department (CDRRMD) said the 11 a.m. heat index was recorded at the Calapan City Public Market monitoring station on Monday. articles.title: Cool Baguio feels the heat: Index nears record at 28°C on May 14
articles.description: BAGUIO CITY — While much of Northern Luzon continues to experience dangerous heat index levels reaching up to 46 degrees Celsius, even the typically cool Baguio City neared its historical peak, with a 28 degrees Celsius on May 14. Weekly data from the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (Pagasa) released on Monday covering May 13 to 17, showed Tuguegarao City again recording the highest heat index in Northern Luzon at 46°C on May 15. The same level was also logged on May 12 and May 14. READ: HEAT INDEX WATCH: Danger levels expected in Metro Manila, 36 others [...]...
